clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Standardized classification of clinical recommendation strength and evidence quality
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Guideline development, clinical decision support, treatment recommendation ranking
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: COR + LOE assignment for clinical recommendations

navigationPath.purpose: Select Class of Recommendation (1, 2a, 2b, 3 No Benefit, 3 Harm)
Display COR strength (STRONG/MODERATE/WEAK) and risk-benefit ratio
Show suggested phrasing for selected COR
Select Level of Evidence (A, B-R, B-NR, C-LD, C-EO)

classOfRecommendation.validation: Must be one of: Class 1, Class 2a, Class 2b, Class 3 No Benefit, Class 3 Harm

levelOfEvidence.validation: Must be one of: A, B-R, B-NR, C-LD, C-EO

validationRules: Any COR may be paired with any LOE

rules: Determine COR based on benefit >> risk (Class 1), benefit >> risk (Class 2a), benefit ≥ risk (Class 2b), benefit = risk (Class 3 No Benefit), risk > benefit (Class 3 Harm)
Assign COR strength: STRONG (Class 1, Class 3 Harm), MODERATE (Class 2a, Class 3 No Benefit), WEAK (Class 2b)
Select suggested phrasing based on COR
Determine LOE based on evidence quality and study design
Combine COR + LOE for final recommendation
Note: LOE C does not imply weak recommendation

categories.name: Class 1
categories.phrasing: Is recommended / Is indicated / Should be performed

categories.name: Class 2b
categories.phrasing: May/Might be reasonable / May/Might be considered

categories.name: Class 3 No Benefit
categories.phrasing: Is not recommended / Is not indicated

evidenceLevels.name: Level A
evidenceLevels.source: >1 RCT, meta-analyses of high-quality RCTs, or RCTs + high-quality registry

evidenceLevels.name: Level B-R
evidenceLevels.source: 1 or more RCTs or meta-analyses of moderate-quality RCTs

evidenceLevels.name: Level B-NR
evidenceLevels.source: 1 or more nonrandomized studies, observational studies, or registry studies

evidenceLevels.name: Level C-LD
evidenceLevels.source: Randomized/nonrandomized studies with design limitations, physiological/mechanistic studies

evidenceLevels.name: Level C-EO
evidenceLevels.source: Consensus of expert opinion based on clinical experience

loeDescription.description: e.g., 'Moderate-quality evidence from 1 or more RCTs'

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Apply classified recommendation to clinical guideline or decision support
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Update when new evidence becomes available
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag Class 3 Harm recommendations as critical safety alerts
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log COR + LOE to clinical decision record
